calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-16-2020 Off Calendar

Defendant Buena Vista Manor'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Serve Summons And Complaint Within Three Years After Commencement Of Action And/Or For Failure To File Proof Of Service Within Statutory Time
Off Calendar Sep-30-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Stephen J. Brooks (415-434-3323; sbrooksowbllp@gmail.com) of O'Donnell Wall Brooks PC for Defendant BUENA VISTA MANOR.
No other appearances.

Not argued. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BUENA VISTA MANOR'S Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Serve Summons And Complaint Within Three Years After Commencement Of Action And/Or For Failure To File Proof Of Service Within Statutory Time is granted.

This action was filed more than five years ago, on May 4, 2015. The record before the court is undisputed that Plaintiff did not serve the defendant with the summons and complaint until September 2019, more than 4 years and 4 months later, and did not file proof of service until September 27, 2019, long after expiration of the 3-year period for service prescribed by Code of Civil Procedure section 583.210(a). Under the circumstances, dismissal is mandatory. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 583.250(a)(2),(b).)

In her late-filed "Objection," Plaintiff contends without evidence that the court extended the time for her to serve the summons and complaint. It did not. To the contrary, the court's October 31, 2018 continued order to show cause for failure to file proof of service, which was served on plaintiff, explicitly warned her: "***NOTICE: Impending Mandatory Dismissal pursuant to CCP Sections 583.210 and 583.250." Plaintiff does not establish that service was impracticable due to causes beyond her control. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 583.240(d).) While the court continued the hearing date on this motion on numerous occasions at plaintiff's request to accommodate her health issues, plaintiff-who prepared and filed numerous such requests and other pleadings-does not establish that she was unable to serve the summons and complaint either herself (for example, by simply filling out and mailing a notice and acknowledgement of receipt form) or by engaging a registered process server.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. There is no Appearance by Plaintiff in person or by phone inspite of the tentative ruling of Hearing Required and that Plaintiff may appear by telephonically via Court Call. Counsel reports he did not receive a copy of the accomodation request. The Court hands/lends its copy of the request to be reviewed by Counsel. The matter is recalled at 9:51 and Counsel returns the Court's copy. Counsel argues. The Court shares its views and grants Plaintiff's accomodation request. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT BUENA VISTA MANOR'S Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Serve Summons And Complaint Within Three Years After Commencement Of Action And/Or For Failure To File Proof Of Service Within Statutory Time.

Plaintiff's request for Accomodations is granted. The motion is continued to 2/20/20. Plaintiff's Opposition due by 2/5/20.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
